campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held their regular meeting on Tuesday, February 3rd, 2026. All council members were present, including Mayor Fat, Vice Mayor Scazola, and Council Members Lopez, Hines, and Bby. The meeting opened with a pledge of allegiance led by Leslie Fabila from the public works department.
keyTopics: Roll call - all council members present
Opening procedures and pledge of allegiance
Public works department participation

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide how to distribute money to community events and programs. They approved which organizations would receive grants, how much each would get, and made some changes to make the grant application rules clearer for future events.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations to receive grant funding
Set individual dollar amounts for each grant recipient
Decided total funding amount for competitive event grants in the fiscal year
Updated and clarified rules for community events and street closure permits

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 3, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 3, 2026, to handle routine business including approving the previous month's meeting notes and signing off on the diversity and inclusion commission's 2026 work plan. The council also heard from a resident named Nimi Kumar and discussed potential changes to how the city formats its official meeting minutes.
keyTopics: Approval of February 17 meeting minutes
2026 Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Commission work plan
Public comment from Nimi Kumar
Possible reforms to meeting minute format

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, the city website, YouTube, phone, or Zoom. The meeting included live translation services in over 50 languages for residents who needed them.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, online, phone, Zoom)
Live translation available in 50+ languages
No public comment period mentioned for this session
Accessible meeting options for residents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council cancelled the zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026. The hearing has been rescheduled for Wednesday, April 8, 2026 instead. If you had plans to discuss zoning or land use matters with the city, you'll need to wait until the new date.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning review delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund housing and community development projects in the city. The meeting included approval of previous meeting notes and presentations from groups seeking funding to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for housing and community projects
Approval of February 25 meeting minutes
Review of Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding requests

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on ways to help local businesses thrive, including a program offering relief to businesses that may have missed license payments in the past. They discussed plans to encourage shopping at local stores through summer campaigns and explored partnerships with private companies to sponsor city events while supporting retail shops and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses catch up on unpaid licenses
Outreach to shopping centers to boost local business participation
Summer Shop Local campaign to encourage residents to buy from Cupertino businesses
Public-Private Partnerships for funding city events
Economic incentives for retail stores, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting to address several postponed items and discuss two main issues: a weed abatement program to deal with overgrown weeds in the city, and plans for how federal housing grant money will be spent in the coming year. Residents had the opportunity to comment on the housing fund allocation.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026 - clearing overgrown properties
Federal HUD housing grant spending plan for 2026/27
Multiple agenda items postponed to future meetings
Public comment period on housing fund allocation

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 25, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss updates on the San Antonio Road area and reviewed changes to building and zoning rules aimed at supporting retail businesses in the city. The council also handled administrative matters like meeting schedules and staff assignments.
keyTopics: San Antonio Road area development and transportation options
New rules to help retail stores thrive in Palo Alto
Updates to building and zoning regulations
City council administrative scheduling
